name: Rashid Shaheed
headline: Field-stretcher carved bigger role, boom-bust WR3 in Seattle
body:
Shaheed posted career-high volume in 2025, playing 18 games on a 90.7 percent route rate and turning a 16.3 percent target share into 59 catches for 687 yards and two scores (97.6 points, WR38). The profile remains explosive and downfield: 11.45 air yards per target, a 22 percent air-yards share and a 0.398 WOPR, with a perfect 0.0 percent drop rate on 92 looks. But efficiency dipped, as a -0.047 EPA per play and 45.7 percent success rate trailed his 2023 Saints form (719 yards, five TDs, 0.161 EPA).

The undrafted Weber State product (rookie 2022) now works a deep Seattle perimeter, where his 11.6 yards per catch and big-play knack give weekly spike potential but a thin floor on a 64.1 percent catch rate. With only nine red-zone targets, his scoring stays volatile. He is a WR3/flex and best-ball favorite whose splash games carry roster-altering ceiling, but inconsistent target quality keeps him out of the must-start tier.
